summary: Ensure min contrast 4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for bold headlines ≥18px; white text on #92278F, #262262, or #01A47E passes.
Avoid silver or periwinkle text on white backgrounds to meet minimum contrast accessibility standards.
All images must include descriptive alt text to ensure accessibility compliance.
Use live-text ISI for email indication and safety; never rely on image-based ISI to ensure accessibility with images off.
Minimum live-text size: footnotes 10 px; body text always above 12 px for accessibility compliance.
Tap targets must be at least 44 px for accessibility.
Use exactly one H1 per email with logical heading order for accessibility compliance.
Underline all links; avoid using color alone as the link indicator for accessibility.
Use semantic <table role="presentation"> markup for layout tables to ensure accessibility.
Each content section is a 600px wide table with its own background, top/bottom padding (§2.2), optional wave edge, no outer margins, stacking flush vertically.
Sections declare top/bottom theme backgrounds to ensure matching or wave transitions; first section meets header on white/theme, last ends background approved against footer wave edge.
Footnote symbols (*) (†) (‡) (§) run continuously email-wide, coordinated at assembly; references numbered continuously across sections, resolving above/within locked End Matter per template.
One Wave graphic and one key-strategic-question callout max per email layout.
Rule (cta): Limit to one primary CTA per section and a maximum of about 3 CTAs in each email.
No new fonts, colors, button shapes, or icon styles outside doc; new icons must match existing line weight and simplified style.
No content section replicates logo lockup or ISI/legal copy; only Top/End Matter sections display these locked elements.
Final assembly QA confirms body size at 12 px/1.4 ISI, audience matches header/footer (§4.2), all §1.8 content present, theme consistent, both breakpoints no horizontal scroll.
Use Purple (#92278F, ~30%, preferred), Dark Blue (#262262, ~30%), White (#FFFFFF, ~20%) as primary colors; Silver, Dark Gray, Green as accents within specified percentages.
Titles (first email line) must use Purple (#92278F) color.
Headlines and subheads use Dark Blue (#262262) color.
Body copy and ISI text must use black color #000000 (correct typo from #00000).
Avoid using Purple (#92278F) or Green (#01A47E) for body copy by rule (color_application) [BASELINE].
Use Reserve Green (#01A47E) exclusively for CTA fills and small accent elements.
Use Dark Gray (#567483) exclusively for charts and secondary UI elements.
Never use Silver (#CFDCE3) color for text (color_application).
Only the title/first line text may be Purple (#92278F); no other text may use Purple color.
Highlight within white body paragraph copy must be black (#000000) and bold; no blue or purple highlight allowed; excludes headings, subheadings, titles.
(color_application): Each sentence, heading or body, must use only one color, with no mixing of two colors within the same sentence.
In Flip The Script campaign, use Periwinkle #7B7BED, Purple #92278F, Green #01A47E at equal 33% each balance.
Flip The Script scene-color pairs fixed: Periwinkle (#7B7BED) with Office, Purple (#92278F) with Cafe, Green (#01A47E) with Soccer; no interchange.
Use Flip The Script colors (Periwinkle #7B7BED, Purple #92278F, Green #01A47E) only as accents in campaign pieces, not replacing the main brand palette.
Callout colors over white: Periwinkle 80% opacity, Purple 60%, Green 40%.
White banner over imagery: set opacity to 80%.
Primary background gradient: Dark Blue #262262 at 0%, midpoint at 40%, Purple #92278F at 100%.
color_application: Silver-to-White gradient (#CFDCE3 at 0%, midpoint at 40%, #FFFFFF at 100%) used for light background/panel.
Use a 45° gradient angle by default, allowing rotation adjustments as necessary.
Apply Dark Blue→Purple gradient sparingly on headlines and callouts, e.g., two-tone headline with back half shifting to Purple.
In email, gradients only as section backgrounds or CSS, with solid #92278F/#262262 fallback for Outlook.
No gradient body text in email; use two-color split headline with navy phrase and purple phrase instead.
Use one theme per email; all sections inherit it; theme variables style frame and restyle tile, button, campaign-image colors automatically.

Approved email theme tiles: White, Silver/light, Purple #92278F, Navy #262262, Green #01A47E; light themes use dark text/teal buttons, dark themes use white text, knockout logo, light/outlined buttons.
Use white text only on Purple (#92278F), Navy (#262262), or Green (#01A47E) panels.
On White/Silver panels, use navy (#262262) headlines, black (#000000) body copy, purple (#92278F) accents.
Links on dark panels (Purple, Navy, Green) must be white and underlined.
Links on light panels (White/Silver) use purple #92278F or navy #262262 and are underlined.

DTP email layout alternates backgrounds: purple hero → white content (efficacy+side-effects) → white/light photo (story/lifestyle) → green #01A47E accent (affordability/icons) → purple campaign image → locked End Matter.
Sections have no edge margin; transitions use background color changes or full-bleed hero images, not dividers or rules.
Green accent panel (#01A47E) appears once per email, reserved for either affordability block or symptom-icon trio, never both stacked.
Green accent panel (#01A47E) limited to affordability block or symptom-icon trio, never stacking both in one green panel.
On light backgrounds (#FFFFFF or #CFDCE3), CTA uses Green filled button (#01A47E) with white label text (#FFFFFF).
On dark #567483, #92278F, #262262, #01A47E, #0F99BC, #7B7BED backgrounds, CTA uses white fill (#FFFFFF) with green label text (#01A47E).
On light backgrounds (#FFFFFF, #CFDCE3), headings use Dark Blue #262262 and body copy uses Black #000000.
On dark backgrounds (#567483, #92278F, #262262, #01A47E, #0F99BC, #7B7BED), headings and body copy must be White #FFFFFF.
CTA section right-side image from three approved options, each locked to a specific CTA background color; select image by content then set matching background color.
Use CTA images: Email_Primary_2_column.png on Purple #92278F; Email_Primary_column_golf.png on Teal #0F99BC if LPGA content; Cake_lockup.png on Periwinkle #7B7BED.
If content section is above CTA, CTA runs image-free with text+button on approved background from lookup table, skipping right-side image.
One image per background: Email_Primary_2_column.png only on non-teal; Email_Primary_column_golf.png only on teal backgrounds.
Use golf image (Email_Primary_column_golf.png) and #0F99BC background only if email contains LPGA content; otherwise, do not use them.
Teal #0F99BC used solely as CTA section background paired with LPGA golf image, not for general backgrounds elsewhere.
Desktop email width 600px, mobile 375px; both layouts required per component; SFMC auto-serves correct layout.
Use library component heights as proportions, not fixed limits: Primary 1-Col 600×1138/375×788; Primary 2-Col 600×1088/375×1538; Secondary 600×458 desktop row.
Use 600px fixed-width table for email layout; apply fluid/hybrid technique collapsing to 375px below 480px screen width for mobile.
Section padding: Outer container side padding 24px desktop, 16px mobile.
Section vertical padding: 32px top/bottom desktop, 24px top/bottom mobile.
No margin between stacked section tables; adjacent sections sit edge-to-edge relying on their own padding for flush backgrounds and wave shapes.
Two-column layouts use 24px gutter on desktop and 24px vertical gap between stacked columns on mobile.
Card internal padding: 16px on all sides.
Spacing rule: Maintain a 16 px gap between an image and adjacent text.
Use a 12-column grid over 552 px content area (600 − 2×24 px); half-width column = 264 px content + 24 px gutter.
All spacing values must be multiples of 8 px; adjust layouts only in 8 px increments, no odd one-off values allowed.
Rounded rectangle callout: white outline, transparent fill, used only for key strategic questions (e.g., "DO YOU WANT TO TRY SOMETHING DIFFERENT?").
Use white banner callout at 80% opacity over background for 'IBSRELA WORKS DIFFERENTLY…' claim.
Use Periwinkle (#7B7BED) at 80% opacity for Office-scene banner callouts over white backgrounds.
Use Purple (#92278F) at 60% opacity for Cafe-scene banner callouts on white backgrounds.
Use Green (#01A47E) at 40% opacity for Soccer-scene banner callouts on white backgrounds.
Scene-paired banner callout colors fixed: Office→Periwinkle 80%, Cafe→Purple 60%, Soccer→Green 40%; no mixing scene and color.
Use approved icon trio ast_ibsrela_symptom_icon_trio for IBS-C symptoms: Constipation, Belly Pain, Bloating left-to-right.
Symptom icons: circular green-tinted; label below in navy #262262 (tok_ibsrela_color_dark_blue).
All three symptom icons must always appear together in original order, never alone or substituted with alternate styles.
Symptom-icon trio placed on green/teal panel or white above it, followed by headline, body copy, and CTA in the layout.
All text including titles, headlines, subheads, body copy, and CTA labels is always left-aligned, regardless of background color.
CTA button aligned left, flush with left edge of the text block above; never centered or right-aligned.
In CTA layout, visuals appear right of text in two columns; no visual means left-aligned, full-width text block.
Headline (H1) uses Avenir Black, Title Case, color Dark Blue #262262 or Purple #92278F.
Subhead (H2): Avenir Black, Sentence case, color Dark Blue #262262 or Purple #92278F.
Body copy uses Avenir, Roman weight, Sentence case, Black #000000 color.
ISI body text: Avenir, Roman, Sentence case, Black #000000.
ISI boxed warning text uses Avenir Heavy font (exclusive), approved ISI case, color Black #000000.
ISI subheads use Avenir Black, all caps, Black #000000 color.
Footnotes/end matter: Avenir, Book, Sentence case, Black #000000.
Campaign headlines use Avenir Black, ALL CAPS, colored by campaign palette.
Callouts and CTAs use Avenir, ALL CAPS; callouts weight Black, CTAs per button spec; color per callout/button specs.
Use Arial as system font replacement for Avenir in emails/PowerPoint; weights map Avenir Black/Heavy to Arial Bold, Roman/Book to Arial Regular (reduce size 1pt for footnotes).
Email fonts use Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; do not load Avenir webfont due to Outlook/SFMC rendering issues; Arial is the brand-approved substitute.
H1 typography: 24px desktop / 22px mobile, line height 1.25, Bold, used for main section headlines like "Prepare for your next appointment...
H2 secondary headline (two-column card) 16px desktop/mobile, line height 1.5, Bold.
Editorial body copy: 16 px desktop/mobile, line height 1.5, Regular; approved digital content body size for emails.
CTA button label: 14px desktop/mobile, line-height 1.2, bold, white pill background, teal (#01A47E) text.
ISI subheads: 14px desktop/mobile, line height 1.4, Bold, used in locked footer build.
ISI "IMPORTANT RISK INFORMATION" heading: 15px font size desktop/mobile, line height 1.4, Bold, in locked footer.
ISI body copy uses 13px size on desktop/mobile, line height 1.4, Regular weight, in the approved locked footer build.
Footnotes, abbreviations, references use 11px font, line height 1.4, Regular weight on desktop/mobile, within locked footer section.
Footer legal unsubscribe copy uses 11px font size, line height 1.4, Regular weight on both desktop and mobile, within locked footer section.
Body copy has 0 letter-spacing; ALL-CAPS callouts and CTAs use +0.5 px tracking; negative tracking prohibited in email.
Spacing in sections: headline→subhead 8px; subhead→body 12px; headline→body (no subhead) 12px; between body paragraphs 12px; body→CTA button 16px.
Editorial default white content sections (intro, copy, options, callbacks) use fixed padding 12px top, 24px right, 12px bottom, 24px left verbatim.
Dark feature sections (navy #262262 / purple #92278F CTA, partnership, closing) use fixed 24px padding all sides (T R B L) exactly.
Inset card/callout in Editorial section uses 12px radius, 24px padding all sides, for boxed highlights in white sections, with border-collapse separate for Outlook.
Side gutters must be 24 px on both edges in every section without exceptions.
Never override vertical padding token; choose Editorial or Dark feature and adjust "air" by section type, not by editing padding values.
Avoid empty spacer rows or placeholder cells; they add 12–16 px and disrupt spacing calculations.
Padding tokens fixed; half-gap per section; Editorial-Editorial=24px; Editorial-Dark=36px; Dark-Dark=48px; full-bleed hero/swirl(0) to section=12px.
End Matter ISI and legal footer fixed system constants; ISI body 13px, editorial content body 16px, content defaults do not restyle these sections.
Headlines use Title Case; all other copy uses sentence case.
Campaign-specific headlines (FLIP THE SCRIPT) must be in ALL CAPS.
Use all caps for callouts and CTAs.
Capitalize each word for campaign names inside body copy, e.g., 'Flip The Script', not all caps or lowercase.
In 'FLIP THE SCRIPT' lockup, 'THE' is upside down; 'ON IBS-C' in Avenir Light 50% opacity, hyphen replaced by 100% opacity brand swoosh (asset ast_ibsrela_flip_the_script_lockup).
Use Arial Bold for headlines, subheads, card titles, CTA labels, ISI subheads, boxed-warning text, and MLR-approved key claim phrases only.
Do not bold entire body paragraphs, footnotes, references, links (underlined only), or over ~10 consecutive words in body copy.
No bold+italic in email body; use Arial Italic only for journal titles in references or per approved copy deck.
Never use bold for hierarchy; promote prominent text to subhead or approved callout style instead.
SFMC emails stack components in order: 1. Top Matter (LOCKED), 2. Primary 1 Column (optional), 3. Primary 2 Column (optional), 4. Secondary (optional, repeatable), 5. End Matter (LOCKED).
Top Matter and End Matter sections are LOCKED and supplied; do not build or modify them.
Select one wireframe template formation; remove unnecessary sections; do not add sections; adjust content to fit template, not vice versa.
Content components must match two reference examples; remove unneeded sections or elements but do not add new element types.
Provide Desktop (600px) and Mobile (375px) layouts for every component; SFMC auto-serves device-appropriate version; QA all content sections at both breakpoints.
First content section abuts locked Top Matter bottom edge (white/theme bg, optional top wave edge); last content section closes against locked End Matter with wave-edge ISI panel below.
IBSRELA logo lockup appears only in locked Top Matter header; never repeat inside content sections; logo allowed only in approved, untouched campaign imagery.
Email's audience set by Top Matter header (DTP: logo without BID, patient-friendly language; HCP: logo with BID); all content copy and imagery must match audience.
Body copy in content sections must use ISI body settings—12 px font size and 1.4 line height—as in the locked End Matter footer (§1.2).
References and footnotes in content sections resolve in/above End Matter; numbering and symbol order must be coordinated at assembly (§4.5).
Avoid duplicating ISI fragments, indication statements, or legal lines inside content sections; place all in locked End Matter.
Email Primary 1 Column: full-width hero image → purple H1 → bold subheadline → body copy → teal "Get connected" CTA; desktop 600×1138 px, mobile 375×788 px, optional wave borders.

Email Primary 2 Column: text left, brand image right; stack on mobile; brand purple gradient background, white text, outlined CTA; desktop ~600×1088 px, mobile ~375×1538 px; editable headline, body, CTA, image; wave shape and button style optional.

Email Secondary layout: two-column repeatable cards with brand image, navy headline, body copy, teal CTA; collapses single-column mobile; desktop ~600×458 px, mobile ~375×1363 px; image/headline/body/CTA editable; toggles: left column on/off, image side, subhead, body, button, images left/right.
Use second person ("you", "your") and focus IBSRELA messaging on returning to everyday life, avoiding clinical performance framing.
Lead with empathetic acknowledgment of unresolved symptoms before introducing product benefit, e.g., "If you're still experiencing…, you don't have to settle for feeling just okay.
Use "IBS-C symptoms" (constipation, belly pain, bloating), never "your condition" or jargon; always list all three symptoms together in benefits.
Patient-story modules require disclosure: "Individual results and experiences may vary. [Name] is an actual compensated IBSRELA patient," placed directly below story copy.
Efficacy claims require qualifier "*Results may vary. Study results seen in 12- and 26-week trials…" and limitation "These results observed but analysis can't draw conclusions.
CTA labels must be short verb phrases, 1-3 words, in sentence case; examples include "Explore IBSRELA", "Watch video", "Learn more about how IBSRELA is different".
Rule (cta): Use a single solid-fill primary CTA style for poll/segmentation question response buttons (e.g., "Yes, I have").
(cta): DTP CTAs cannot use HCP library default "Get connected" as that label is restricted to HCP scope only.
(cta): Section CTAs must match topic: patient-story → "Watch [name]'s story"; product overview → "Explore IBSRELA" or "Learn more about how IBSRELA is different".
